purpose: Companion assumption block for the F1-K REVISION-6 GEOMETRY freeze package: the maintainer-approved powered geometry (2026-07-15) for the programme's DECIDING four-condition kernel-vs-generic CORRECTNESS test (F1-K rungs K-1/K-2/K-3), applied to registry/experiments/f1k.json (DRAFT, kot-reg/1) + the re-pinned analysis script analysis/f1k.py + the new pinned corpus data/f1k-contrast-v1/. EMITTED by the Fable experiment-designer (designer-32, 2026-07-15) after the $0 blind askability screen (designer-31, same day) returned NOT-ASKABLE-AT-SCALE at the prior n=1440 cap by a power knife-edge (K-3 joint power 0.7955 vs the 0.80 gate) — the registered PRE-RUN RETURN — and the maintainer ruled to raise the cap. Central registration in registry/assumptions.jsonl is the COORDINATOR's action with the landing commit. This pass takes NO git action and states NO feasibility conclusion. Range verified free at emission: central register holds nothing at or above 2369; repo-wide grep confirms 2369..2375 unused.
backing_artifacts: poc/f1k-askability/power_n1573.py (the confirmation harness; sole change vs screen.py Part 3 is N_TEST 1440->1573)

entries.claim: F1-K REVISION-6 powered geometry, maintainer-approved 2026-07-15; SUCCESSOR of ASM-2271 (supersede-by-citation of the C>=65 each-m>=8 at n<=1440 gate): the F1-K test set is EXACTLY n = 1,573 items over EXACTLY C = 96 concept clusters (the frozen askability-screen selection, ranks 1..96, 45 kernel-v0 + 51 kernel-v1; redacted-input hash 4f7cf1c6a5b5e92655581ba901ea35ce3c6781e3f5279a7b4181b2d4bafc0359 frozen before screening), each cluster m >= 8 (realized m_min 10, m_mean 16.3854, m_max 18 under the [BC] OP-6 allocation: dev-96 round-robin, test breadth-first to m=8, round-robin to the cap), pre-registered effect mu* = +4.09 pts, replication R-vector (1,3,1) for K-1/K-2/K-3. The power gate becomes: >= 96 clusters EACH with >= 8 test items at exactly n = 1,573 (analysis/f1k.py rejects any other n fail-closed). The prior n_max = 1440 (SSR-REV3.1 item 4) is superseded by this explicit maintainer ruling; the askability screen's NOT-ASKABLE-AT-SCALE verdict at n=1440 stands as the honest record of WHY the cap moved: the geometry was re-powered, not the bar lowered — mu*, the +3-pt floor, alpha, seeds and the fire rule are byte-identical.

K-3 CO-PRIMARY promotion (the deciding four-condition test): K-1 (K vs b0), K-2 (K vs the per-item mean over R=3 seeded derangements of the identical carrier set — the shuffled-kernel control, condition iii by construction) and K-3 (K vs the matched plain-dictionary d2 knull — the kernel-vs-generic contrast, condition iv certified at NLD>=0.20 min 0.387) are CO-PRIMARY: the record VERDICT PASS requires all three joint passes (pass_gate = k1_joint_pass AND k2_joint_pass AND k3_joint_pass; fire rule per rung unchanged: exact cluster sign-flip p < 0.05 AND observed lift >= +3.0 pts, i.e. T >= 0.03). A K~d2 tie — previously the modal expectation that 'elevates wording, never the verdict' (f1k pre-REVISION-6) — now DENIES the PASS and is reported at equal prominence as the content-not-structure datum (ladder rung 2). The kot-reg/1 single-primary lint is satisfied structurally: the schema-designated role:primary endpoint remains K-1 (it carries the SESOI/TOST), and the conjunction lives in /analysis/pass_gate which the PASS verdict rule reads; K-2/K-3 carry role:secondary with CO-PRIMARY status declared in their registered test text. Each rung retains exactly one primary contrast and one fire rule.

Frozen-geometry exact-power confirmation (poc/f1k-askability/power_n1573.py, 2026-07-15, $0/blind/deterministic; the pre-registered SSR-REV5 procedure byte-identical to screen.py Part 3, seed 20260713, N_sim=10000, B=10000 add-one-corrected sign-flips, delta=rho_U=0.10, fire iff p<0.05 AND T>=+3 pts): at C=96, n=1573, mu*=+4.09 pts — K-1 (R=1) 0.8043, K-2 (R=3) 0.8058, K-3 (R=1) 0.8001, MC-SE ~0.004 each; 80%-power MDEs 4.072/4.063/4.090 pts. All three rungs >= 0.80: the maintainer-approved geometry is CONFIRMED powered under the frozen procedure and matches the approval memo figures (0.804/0.806/0.800). DISCLOSED KNIFE-EDGE: K-3 clears by 0.0001 at MC-SE 0.0040 — the frozen-seed point estimate passes the pre-registered rule, but true K-3 power may sit marginally either side of 0.80; the +4.09-pt MDE wording in power_scope (not a 'well-powered' claim) is the honest scope of any K-3 non-fire.

The pre-spend 'structure-question-askable' deflator gate is SATISFIED: the $0, gold-label-blind, model-outcome-blind askability screen (designer-31, 2026-07-15; redacted-input hash frozen BEFORE screening 4f7cf1c6a5b5e92655581ba901ea35ce3c6781e3f5279a7b4181b2d4bafc0359, rules hash cc14544d3ca7bb80ef0c43ae721a33ccbfeeff3c98a2f6a1caf61564de66ad55) established BEFORE any authoring or GPU spend that (a) 96 concepts with existing kernel records realize m>=8 gate-eligible clusters over the 5 pinned benchmarks (19,311 redacted items), and (b) condition iv (kernel-vs-generic distinctness) is CERTIFIED for all 96: per-pair hashes differ, NLD >= 0.20 (min 0.387, median 0.738), production-prepend prompt-hash-difference rate 1.00, outside-payload differences 0 (data/f1k-contrast-v1/, ranks 097-116 = the fixed 20-deep reserve). The screen's own n=1440 power verdict NOT-ASKABLE-AT-SCALE was returned to the maintainer and resolved by the ASM-2369 geometry ruling, not overridden by this designer.

entries.resolution_path: human fidelity validation pass over the 96 pinned explication texts of data/f1k-contrast-v1/ ranks 001-096 (explication-to-concept faithfulness judged by human annotators under a pre-registered instrument, m0a-discipline: blind rendering, deranged-explication probe, retest floor); until then every F1-K PASS citation carries the PROXY-PROVISIONAL label
entries.claim: CONDITION-I VALIDITY BAR (stated so a PASS cannot be overclaimed): the 96 F1-K explications are drawn from existing kernel-v0 records (explicator-authored through the encoder/validator loop) and kernel-v1 records (pipeline-minted, stage A). Their validation to date is MECHANICAL (kot-ast/1 parse, encoder round-trip, molecule/typing checks) plus LLM-PROXY passes elsewhere in the programme; NO human fidelity validation of explication-to-concept faithfulness exists for these texts (the m0a human pass — the programme's standing gold standard for annotation-instrument claims — remains outstanding). The explication instrument is therefore PROXY/UNVALIDATED, not a VALIDATED INSTRUMENT. Consequence, written into the f1k record and envelope: a full F1-K PASS (all three co-primary rungs fire) licenses 'THESE 96 kernel-record texts, as carriers, beat matched dictionary glosses on concept-covered QA at this model/box' — a PROXY-PROVISIONAL kernel-methodology result. It does NOT license 'validated NSM explications beat dictionaries' until the human fidelity pass confirms the texts are faithful explications rather than merely different, possibly better-engineered, prompt/carrier content. The kernel-vs-generic scoreboard entry from a PASS is PROXY-PROVISIONAL for the same reason.

REVISION-6 budget; SUCCESSOR of ASM-2283 (supersede-by-citation of the $149 ceiling, itself successor of ASM-2277): budget.usd_cap = $155, ~555 instance-hours, 0 GPU-h, maintainer-approved 2026-07-15 with the geometry. Derivation at the ASM-2205 pessimistic corner (100 s/prefill / 1.20 pinning speedup / $0.28/h spot i4i.2xlarge): main 8 passes x 1,573 = 12,584 prefills + construction <= 3,072 + pilot ~6,200 + guard <= 660 = 22,516 prefills -> 521.2 h -> $145.9 WITHOUT the conditional REPLACE arm; WITH REPLACE (+1,573 prefills) 24,089 -> 557.6 h -> ~$156 at the same corner, marginally above the cap. Pre-registered resolution (consistent with SSR-REV4.3's PRE-test run/defer and the SSR6 ordering): REPLACE runs ONLY if the bring-up-measured throughput projection keeps the total ledger <= $155 — otherwise REPLACE is DEFERRED (its registered modal expectation) with no effect on the three co-primary rungs. The cap is a cap, not a target; ASM-2205's throughput figures remain the open extrapolation they were, resolved only by the metered run at the bring-up affordability gate.

Input pins completing freeze-manifest (A) item (ii) PRE-SPEND: the kernel-explication and d2 dictionary-definition TEXTS per concept are no longer PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholders deferred to the ops amendment — they are pinned NOW as data/f1k-contrast-v1/ (kot-corpus-hash/1 c6eb82944ed15827d15aae59e7e522693b1a9917cd914086e8514778a47fb78d; 116 concept dirs x {kernel.txt, kernel.ast.json JCS bytes, dictionary.txt} + README), byte-identical to the screen-certified artifacts. The source corpora are pinned alongside: kernel-v0 8209cadabcfc2eaa11631c5c1100c04a48f33673516780b1f36cbf957217c809 (already in the record, reproduces) and kernel-v1 f2c8472778bc95f92c09b95f845b8d32e383b980d62cb9e801693cd8066a6fd1 (NEW pin — 51 of the 96 concepts are kernel-v1). The remaining (A) items (contexts, layers, formulas, seeds, trigger map, template, id lists) stay pre-declared ops amendments in the registered order; d3-text and K construction MUST read the explication bytes from this pin (kernel.txt), and d2 MUST read dictionary.txt — any divergence is a logged protocol amendment before spend, never silent.
